title: trisetum spicatum subsp. majus
intro: Trisetum spicatum subsp. majus, also known as the tall oatgrass or big oatgrass, is a perennial grass native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its tall stems and flat, open panicles. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene and flavonoid compounds. The health benefits of Trisetum spicatum subsp. majus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, as well as being used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
